content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 7, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 7, 2026, focused on planning for enhanced agent security and autonomy. The project received an 'A' security grade for its MCP server and initiated discussions regarding the implementation of delegation chains to manage autonomous agent authority and spending limits.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*No code changes, features, or refactoring were completed during this period.*

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#6709](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/6709): Fix/toon action params.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*None.*

## NEW ISSUES

### Security and Infrastructure
*   [#6710](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6710): The eliza MCP server received an 'A' security grade from Loaditout, successfully passing all 7 automated security criteria.

### Agent Autonomy and Governance
*   [#6711](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6711): Proposal to implement delegation chains for autonomous agents to move beyond binary trust models. This includes scoped authority, defined spend limits, and cascade revocation mechanisms.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*None.*
